title: CRITICAL: Monthly billing batch ABORT - BILLINK DB link failure
description: BILL_080_SYNC_CRM job aborted at 02:47. DB link BILLINK to Amdocs Oracle DB timed out. Billing batch stopped at step 8/10. ~48,000 customers have invoices generated but NOT synced to CRM. Customer360 screen shows stale balance for affected customers.
root_cause: Amdocs DB server rebooted for unplanned patching by Windows team at 02:30. No change freeze notification to Billing ops team. DB link connection pool exhausted waiting for reconnect.
resolution: Amdocs server came back online 04:55. Manual BILL_080_SYNC_CRM re-run initiated 05:00. Completed 05:22. Business impact: 2.5 hour window of stale CRM data.
action_items: CON-2024-0031: Implement DB link health check before batch start
CON-2024-0032: Add change freeze notification to Billing ops distribution list
recurrence_note: 3rd BILLINK failure in 6 months. Root cause pattern: tight coupling via DB link with no circuit breaker or async fallback.

title: DEADLOCK: Order submission failure - CRM/Billing circular dependency
description: Order submission rate dropped to 0 at 14:23. CRM agents reporting 'Order submission failed - system timeout' for all new orders. IVR order channel also down. Retail POS showing timeout on product validation.
root_cause: Flash sale promotion started at 14:00 drove 8x normal order volume. SP_SUBMIT_ORDER calls Oracle Billing PKG_BILLING.VALIDATE_PRODUCT synchronously. VALIDATE_PRODUCT calls back to Amdocs CUSTOMER_MASTER via reverse DB link to check customer eligibility. Under high load, session pool exhausted on both sides creating mutual wait. Classic distributed deadlock.
resolution: Emergency restart of both Amdocs app server and Oracle Billing connection pool at 16:00. Order queue drained manually over following 4 hours. 340 orders lost - required manual re-entry.
action_items: ARCH-2023-0089: Break circular synchronous dependency between CRM and Billing - P1 architectural risk
SHORT-TERM: Add rate limiting on order submission endpoint
recurrence_note: First occurrence of this specific deadlock but the circular dependency has been flagged in architecture reviews since 2021.

title: Netcracker work order sync failure - ghost orders accumulating
description: Provisioning team reported 847 orders in Netcracker in IN_PROGRESS state with no corresponding activity. Orders show in CRM as COMPLETED but Netcracker has no work order ID assigned (NC_WORK_ORDER_ID NULL). Services show as active in CRM billing but no physical resource assigned in Netcracker.
root_cause: SP_SUBMIT_ORDER makes synchronous SOAP call to Netcracker. If Netcracker responds after 10s timeout, CRM marks order as submitted but does not receive/store the NC_WORK_ORDER_ID. Netcracker creates the work order but CRM never learns the ID. These become orphaned.
resolution: Wrote ad-hoc reconciliation script matching on CUST_ID + SUBMITTED_DT +/- 30s to correlate orphaned orders. 847 NC_WORK_ORDER_IDs backfilled. 23 orders had no Netcracker counterpart and were resubmitted.
action_items: CON-2024-0088: Implement async order correlation job to detect and heal NC_WORK_ORDER_ID gaps daily
recurrence_note: This class of issue occurs monthly due to Netcracker latency spikes. Estimated 200-400 ghost orders accumulate per month.

title: SVC_ORDER_AUDIT table causing billing report timeout - 94M rows
description: Monthly order volume report for April failed. DBA trace shows full table scan on SVC_ORDER_AUDIT (94M rows, no partitioning). Query running for 47 minutes before timeout. Management dashboard showing blank for April metrics.
root_cause: SVC_ORDER_AUDIT has no partitioning and no archival strategy. 15 years of audit data accumulated. Report query joins to ORDER table on ORDER_ID with date filter but optimizer choosing full scan due to statistics staleness.
resolution: DBA rebuilt statistics. Query now using IDX_SO_DATES index. Runtime reduced to 4.2 minutes. Workaround only.
action_items: TECH-2024-0201: Implement range partitioning on SVC_ORDER_AUDIT by CHANGE_DT
TECH-2024-0202: Define and implement audit table retention policy (recommend 3 years online, 7 years archive)
recurrence_note: Table grows ~8M rows/month. Without partitioning, will become unmanageable within 12 months.

title: Emergency: Product code FIBER_1G_RES retired in billing but still active in CRM subscriptions
description: Product FIBER_1G_RES was set to RETIRED in Oracle Billing PRODUCT_CATALOG. However Amdocs has no FK enforcement to billing product catalog - only application-layer validation. 2,340 active subscriptions in CUSTOMER_PRODUCT still reference this retired product code. Billing batch is generating invoices with RETIRED product but not flagging for review.
root_cause: No automated sync between Oracle Billing product catalog status and Amdocs CRM. Product retirement process documented in Confluence does not include Amdocs notification step. Process gap.
resolution: Data fix: 2,340 subscriptions migrated to replacement product FIBER_1G_RES_V2 via emergency change. Process update: Added Amdocs team to product sunset approval chain.
action_items: PROC-2024-0044: Implement product catalog sync job between Oracle Billing and Amdocs

title: Customer 360 screen performance - P95 latency 4.2 seconds
description: Call center agents reporting Customer360 screen taking 4-8 seconds to load. AHT (Average Handling Time) metrics showing degradation. Estimated 12 seconds wasted per customer interaction.
root_cause: SP_CUSTOMER_360 makes 3 synchronous calls in sequence: (1) Netcracker REST /resources, (2) Oracle Billing PKG_INVOICE.GET_BALANCE - queries unpartitioned PAYMENT table, (3) CRM_MKTG segment lookup. No parallelization, no caching. P99 Netcracker response 1.8s, Oracle Billing 3.2s.
resolution: Short-term: DBA tuned PKG_INVOICE.GET_BALANCE index. Latency reduced to 4.2s P95. Long-term fix requires architectural change to async/cached customer context service.
action_items: ARCH-2024-0112: Design customer context caching layer (Redis) for CRM read operations
impact_note: With 450 call center agents, 4.2s avg screen load = ~90 minutes of agent time wasted per day across the center.

title: PCI-DSS Finding: Raw payment processor responses contain partial card data
description: Annual PCI-DSS audit identified PAYMENT.PROCESSOR_RESPONSE column contains raw payment gateway responses from pre-2018 era that include truncated PAN data (last 4 digits + BIN). Column not masked, not encrypted, accessible to BILLING_APP database user which is shared across multiple application components.
root_cause: Pre-2018 payment processor integration stored raw response for debugging. Post-2018 processor was replaced and responses sanitized. Historical records never cleaned. Data retained per default 7-year policy without PCI review.
resolution: OPEN - Remediation plan: (1) Mask/delete PANs from historical PROCESSOR_RESPONSE records, (2) Restrict column access to PCI-designated accounts only, (3) Add column encryption.
compliance_risk: HIGH - PCI-DSS Requirement 3.4 violation. Potential for Level 1 audit finding if not remediated before next assessment.

title: GDPR Risk: CONSENT_FLAG NULL treated as consent=granted in CRM
description: Privacy audit revealed CONTACT_DETAILS.CONSENT_FLAG is NULL for 2.1M contact records (31% of total). Application code in CRM treats NULL as Y (consent given). Marketing batch jobs using these contacts for promotional campaigns. Legal flagged as potential GDPR Article 6 violation (no valid lawful basis for processing without explicit consent record).
root_cause: CONSENT_FLAG column added May 2018 for GDPR compliance. Migration script set existing records to NULL rather than requiring explicit re-consent. Application code defaulted to NULL=granted for business continuity.
resolution: OPEN - Legal review in progress. Technical remediation options: (1) Null-to-N migration with re-consent campaign, (2) Treat NULL as N immediately (will exclude 2.1M contacts from marketing).
compliance_risk: HIGH - GDPR violation. Potential regulatory fine.
